title: The August Revolution of 1945
summary: The lightning-fast general uprising across the country, breaking the chains of slavery and reclaiming power for the people.

slides.text: Hey kids! In August 1945, when the opportunity came, Uncle Ho ordered the General Uprising. Everywhere, our people rose up simultaneously to seize power.
slides.imagePrompt: People in a village street holding red flags with yellow stars, rushing towards a government building, sunset, energy and hope, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: In Hanoi, tens of thousands of people flocked to occupy the Kham Sai Palace (Phủ Khâm sai). The morale was soaring, with shouts of 'Independent Vietnam' echoing through the streets.
slides.imagePrompt: Masses of people in front of an old French-style building (Phủ Khâm sai), waving flags, diverse group of workers and peasants, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: In just 15 days, from Hue to Saigon, colonial and feudal rule was completely dismantled. The people truly became the masters of their country.
slides.imagePrompt: A large chain being broken by a golden light, background showing landmarks of Hue and Saigon, symbolic victory, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: The victory of the August Revolution opened a new era, preparing for the birth of the Democratic Republic of Vietnam.
slides.imagePrompt: A new sun rising over a peaceful Vietnamese landscape, people cheering, sense of a fresh beginning, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: In what year did the August General Uprising take place?
From whom did the August Revolution reclaim power?
